content:
{
  "title": "Chain-of-Verification: Interleaving Fact-Checking Prompts to Suppress LLM Hallucination",
  "problem": "Large language models frequently generate plausible-sounding but factually incorrect content, i.e., hallucinations, especially in open-ended question answering and summarization. Existing prompting strategies like chain-of-thought improve reasoning but do not reliably reduce factual errors.",
  "motivation": "Reducing hallucinations is critical for the safe deployment of LLMs in high-stakes and knowledge-intensive applications. Current advances in prompting have not systematically incorporated real-time verification, and the rapid evolution of LLMs makes lightweight, generalizable interventions urgent and valuable.",
  "method": "We propose a 'Chain-of-Verification' prompting scheme, where each major factual claim generated by the LLM is immediately followed by a secondary prompt instructing the model to fact-check its own previous statement before proceeding. This process alternates generation and verification steps, requiring the model to explicitly justify or retract each claim. The prompt format is modular and can be appended to any factual generation task without external tools.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will test the method on a subset of the TruthfulQA and HotpotQA datasets, comparing vanilla, chain-of-thought, and our chain-of-verification prompts using GPT-3.5 and GPT-4. Automatic factuality metrics and human annotation will be used to assess reduction in hallucination rates and answer quality. Statistical significance of improvements will be measured against baseline prompting methods."
}
